explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a set of 17 global goals adopted by all United Nations member states in 2015 to end poverty, protect the planet, and ensure prosperity for all by 2030. Achieving these ambitious goals is impossible for any single country or organization working alone. It requires cooperation at every level: within a country, different government ministries, businesses, and civil society must work together (national cooperation). At the same time, countries must share knowledge, technology, and resources across borders (international cooperation). This combined effort is what makes progress on the SDGs possible. While partnership is a means to this end, and capability enhancement is a result, the ultimate objective that begins with cooperation is obtaining the SDGs.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which one is considered as the complete document for women rights and coordinates among various adopted issues to mitigate discrimination against women?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"CEDAW convention","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"UNIFEM","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"Charter for women rights","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"Charter for elimination of discriminations","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW) is often described as the most comprehensive international treaty on women's rights. Adopted in 1979, it brings together various principles and standards that were previously scattered across different declarations and conventions. CEDAW defines what constitutes discrimination against women and sets an agenda for national action to end such discrimination. It covers civil, political, economic, social, and cultural rights, and it establishes a monitoring committee to review countries' progress. Unlike UNIFEM, which was an operational fund, or generic charters, CEDAW is a legally binding treaty that coordinates all aspects of women's rights under one framework. This is why it is considered the complete document for women's rights.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: When did the CEDAW convention come into effect?
explanation: There is an important difference between when a treaty is adopted and when it comes into effect. CEDAW was adopted by the UN General Assembly in 1979, meaning the text was finalized and countries could start signing it. However, for it to become binding international law, a certain number of countries had to ratify it—formally agree to be legally bound by its terms. The convention specified that it would enter into force 30 days after the 20th country ratified it. This milestone was achieved in 1981. Specifically, CEDAW came into effect on September 3, 1981. From that date onward, countries that ratified it were obligated to align their laws and policies with CEDAW's provisions and report regularly on their progress. So, the correct year for when it became effective is 1981.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: UNIFEM is working to — i. make the women self-dependent ii. ensure safe working place for women iii. develop science and education system Which one is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":false}]
explanation: UNIFEM, which stands for the United Nations Development Fund for Women, was established to promote women's empowerment and gender equality. Its work focused on two main areas relevant to this question. First, it aimed to make women self-dependent by supporting their economic participation—through microfinance, skills training, and entrepreneurship programs. Second, it worked to ensure safe working places for women by advocating for laws against workplace harassment and promoting decent work standards. While education is important for empowerment, developing the broader science and education system was not UNIFEM's primary mandate; that falls more under UNESCO. In 2011, UNIFEM was merged into UN Women, which continues this work. Therefore, statements i and ii correctly describe UNIFEM's focus.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background of Establishing the United Nations
question: In which war were the human rights violated?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"World War I","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"World War II","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"World War I & World War II","is_correct":true},{"label":"d","text":"Vietnam War","is_correct":false}]
explanation: Human rights violations occurred in many conflicts, but the two World Wars stand out for their scale and systematic nature. In World War I (1914-1918), there were widespread atrocities against civilians, use of chemical weapons, and harsh treatment of prisoners of war. World War II (1939-1945) saw even more horrific violations, including the Holocaust, mass bombings of cities, forced labor, and war crimes on an unprecedented scale. These atrocities directly motivated the creation of the United Nations and the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 1948. While the Vietnam War also involved human rights abuses, the question asks about the wars that led to the global human rights framework, which are both World Wars. Therefore, the correct answer is that human rights were violated in both World War I and World War II.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: To achieve sustainable development goal, it is require — i. to work with the whole nation unitedly ii. to develop global relationship iii. to keep coordination among all efforts Which one is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":true}]
explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are complex and interconnected, so achieving them requires a multi-layered approach. First, within a country, all sectors—government, businesses, civil society, and citizens—must work together with a shared vision. This national unity ensures policies are consistent and resources are used effectively. Second, no country can solve global challenges like climate change or pandemics alone. Developing strong global relationships allows for sharing technology, finance, and best practices across borders. Third, even with national and international efforts, progress can be wasted if different projects work in isolation. Coordination among all efforts prevents duplication, aligns priorities, and creates synergy. Since all three elements—national unity, global relationships, and coordination—are essential, the correct answer is d) i, ii and iii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background of Establishing the United Nations
question: In which year did Bangladesh obtain the United Nations' membership?
explanation: After gaining independence in 1971, Bangladesh applied for membership in the United Nations. However, its admission was delayed due to geopolitical complexities, including objections from some permanent members of the Security Council. After extensive diplomatic efforts and growing international recognition of Bangladesh, the UN Security Council recommended Bangladesh for membership. Finally, on September 17, 1974, the UN General Assembly voted to admit Bangladesh as the 136th member state of the United Nations. This membership was a major milestone for Bangladesh, giving it a platform to participate in global decision-making, access development assistance, and contribute to international peace and cooperation. Therefore, the correct year is 1974.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background of Establishing the United Nations
question: After the World War II, which organization was established due to necessity?
explanation: World War II ended in 1945, leaving the world devastated and determined to prevent another such catastrophe. The League of Nations, created after World War I, had failed to stop aggression and maintain peace. Recognizing this failure, leaders from allied nations met to design a new, stronger international organization. The result was the United Nations, founded on October 24, 1945. Its Charter emphasized collective security, human rights, and economic cooperation as essential tools for lasting peace. The Commonwealth is a voluntary association of former British colonies, established earlier. The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) was founded in 1969. Therefore, the organization established out of necessity after WWII was the United Nations.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: After how many years of the 3rd World Conference on Women was the 4th World Conference on Women held?
explanation: The United Nations has held four major world conferences on women to advance gender equality. The Third World Conference on Women took place in Nairobi, Kenya, in 1985. It focused on strategies for achieving equality, development, and peace. Ten years later, the Fourth World Conference on Women was held in Beijing, China, in 1995. This conference produced the landmark Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action, which remains a key global policy framework for women's empowerment. The gap between 1985 and 1995 is exactly 10 years. These decadal conferences allowed time to assess progress, learn from experiences, and set new, more ambitious goals. Therefore, the correct answer is 10 years.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: For which ministry was Humayun Rashid Chowdhury a minister in 1986?
explanation: Humayun Rashid Chowdhury was a distinguished Bangladeshi diplomat and politician. In 1986, he served as the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Bangladesh. This role placed him at the forefront of representing Bangladesh on the global stage. That same year, he was elected President of the 41st Session of the United Nations General Assembly, a prestigious position that rotates among regional groups. As Foreign Minister and UNGA President, he played a key role in advancing issues important to developing countries, such as disarmament, development, and peaceful resolution of disputes. His leadership highlighted Bangladesh's growing diplomatic presence. Therefore, the ministry he led in 1986 was Foreign Affairs.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which associate organization of the UN works for children rights?
explanation: The United Nations has several specialized agencies, each with a specific focus. UNICEF, which stands for the United Nations Children's Fund, is the agency mandated to protect and promote the rights of every child, everywhere. Its work includes providing vaccines, nutrition, education, clean water, and protection from violence and exploitation. UNICEF operates in over 190 countries and is often the first responder in emergencies affecting children. UNIFEM (now part of UN Women) focuses on women's empowerment, UNESCO on education, science, and culture, and WHO on global public health. While all these agencies contribute to children's well-being indirectly, UNICEF has the exclusive mandate for children's rights. Therefore, the correct answer is UNICEF.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: How many articles are in the CEDAW convention?
explanation: The Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW) is structured into 30 articles. These articles are grouped into several parts. Articles 1 to 16 define discrimination and outline specific rights in political, economic, social, and cultural life. Articles 17 to 22 establish the Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women, which monitors implementation. Articles 23 to 30 cover administrative and procedural matters, such as how countries ratify the convention and resolve disputes. This comprehensive structure ensures that CEDAW addresses both the principles and practical steps needed to achieve gender equality. Therefore, the total number of articles in the CEDAW convention is 30.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In 1960, the UN approved the elimination of discrimination against women in— i. Education ii. Employment iii. Professionalism Which one of the following is the correct?
explanation: Before the comprehensive CEDAW convention in 1979, the UN addressed women's rights through specific conventions. In 1960, two important instruments were adopted. First, the UNESCO Convention against Discrimination in Education called for equal access to education for girls and women. Second, the International Labour Organization (ILO) adopted conventions focused on eliminating discrimination in employment and occupation, ensuring equal pay and opportunities. While "professionalism" is related to employment, it was not a separate category in these 1960 agreements. The focus was squarely on education and employment as foundational areas for women's empowerment. Therefore, the correct statements are i and ii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which of the following do the activities of chairman point to?
explanation: The question refers to a scenario where a chairman is involved in activities like building clinics (for health), installing tube-wells (for clean water), and constructing schools (for education). These activities directly align with several specific Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). For example, clinics relate to SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-being), tube-wells to SDG 6 (Clean Water and Sanitation), and schools to SDG 4 (Quality Education). The Millennium Development Goals (MDGs) were the predecessors to the SDGs and ended in 2015. While healthy lives and human rights are important, they are components within the broader SDG framework. Since the activities cover multiple interconnected goals under the 2030 Agenda, they point most comprehensively to the SDGs.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In which group should the general people be included in the partnership method?
explanation: The partnership approach to development, especially for achieving the SDGs, emphasizes that everyone has a role to play. General people—meaning ordinary citizens, community members, and local groups—are not just passive beneficiaries; they are active participants in the development process. When included as development workers, they contribute local knowledge, ensure projects meet real needs, and help sustain outcomes. This participatory model leads to more effective and equitable development. Politicians, rich people, and government workers are also important partners, but the question specifically asks about the role for general people. In the partnership framework, they are empowered as development workers, making option a the correct choice.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Mr. Hanoy is working with —
explanation: Based on the context of Bangladesh and UN specialized agencies, Mr. Hanoy is likely involved in work related to refugee protection. The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) is the UN agency mandated to safeguard the rights and well-being of refugees, asylum-seekers, and stateless persons. In Bangladesh, UNHCR has been actively supporting Rohingya refugees who fled from Myanmar, providing shelter, food, healthcare, and legal protection. While UNDP focuses on development, UNIFEM on women's issues, and UNESCO on education and culture, the specific mention of a name in this context points to humanitarian refugee work. Therefore, Mr. Hanoy is working with UNHCR.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: What is the main objective of the UN?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"To maintain international peace and security","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"To stop war","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"To strengthen international economy","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"To resolve food crisis","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The United Nations Charter, which is the founding treaty of the organization, clearly states its purposes in Article 1. The very first purpose is "to maintain international peace and security." This means preventing conflicts, helping parties in dispute to settle peacefully, and taking collective action against threats to peace. While stopping war (option b) is part of this, it is a narrower goal. Strengthening the international economy and resolving food crises are important tasks handled by other UN bodies like ECOSOC, FAO, or WFP, but they are secondary to the primary objective of peace and security. All UN activities ultimately support this main goal because lasting development and human rights are impossible without peace. Therefore, the correct answer is a.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: The road of Ratul's house is being re-constructed. Which group of people will be benefitted from this?
explanation: Infrastructure projects like road reconstruction have wide-ranging benefits that extend to the entire community. When a road is improved, it becomes safer and easier for everyone to travel. Children can get to school more reliably, patients can reach hospitals faster, farmers can transport goods to markets, and businesses can receive supplies efficiently. While specific groups like the poor, politicians, or businessmen may gain particular advantages, the nature of public infrastructure is that it is non-excludable—once built, everyone in the area can use it. This inclusive benefit aligns with the SDG principle of "leaving no one behind." Therefore, the reconstruction of Ratul's house road will benefit every group of people in the community.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Apart from Rohingya, which one below is an important concern at Bangladesh for UNHCR?
explanation: UNHCR, the UN Refugee Agency, works in Bangladesh primarily to protect and assist refugees. While the Rohingya crisis is the most prominent, UNHCR has also been involved with another long-standing group: the "Stranded Pakistanis," often referred to as Biharis or Bharis. These are people who moved to East Pakistan (now Bangladesh) before 1971 but were left stateless after Bangladesh's independence. Many have lived in camps for decades without citizenship or basic rights. UNHCR has advocated for their legal status, access to services, and durable solutions. Tribal groups and Buddhist people are communities within Bangladesh, not typically under UNHCR's refugee mandate. The Arakan Army is a non-state actor in Myanmar. Therefore, apart from Rohingya, the Stranded Bharis are an important concern for UNHCR in Bangladesh.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Why is it necessary to adjoin the separate activities?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"to implement the SDGs","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"to implement the MDGs","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"to enhance partnership","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"to develop social connection","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are designed as an integrated framework, meaning the goals are interconnected. For example, improving education (SDG 4) affects health outcomes (SDG 3) and economic growth (SDG 8). If different organizations or government departments work on these issues in isolation, efforts can be duplicated, resources wasted, or even contradictory policies created. By adjoining—or coordinating—separate activities, stakeholders can align their strategies, share data, and create synergies. This integrated approach is essential for effectively implementing the SDGs, which aim for holistic progress. While enhancing partnership and social connections are beneficial, they are means to the end goal of SDG implementation. The MDGs were the previous framework, now succeeded by the SDGs. Therefore, the primary reason to adjoin activities is to implement the SDGs.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Married women will be able to keep or change their nationality. When was this provision declared?
explanation: Before the mid-20th century, many countries had laws that automatically changed a woman's nationality when she married a foreigner, often making her stateless or forcing her to adopt her husband's nationality. To address this injustice, the United Nations adopted the Convention on the Nationality of Married Women in 1957. This treaty established that marriage should not automatically affect a woman's nationality. Instead, married women should have the same rights as men to retain, acquire, or change their nationality. This was a significant step toward gender equality in citizenship laws. The convention entered into force in 1958. Therefore, the provision was declared in 1957.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Bangladesh is currently working to achieve how many goals of Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)?
explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a universal call to action adopted by all United Nations member states in 2015. There are exactly 17 goals, ranging from ending poverty and hunger to ensuring quality education, gender equality, clean energy, and climate action. Bangladesh, as a committed UN member, has integrated these 17 goals into its national development plans, such as the Eighth Five-Year Plan and Perspective Plan 2041. The government, along with development partners and civil society, is working on all 17 goals, though progress varies by target. It's important to note that the question asks about goals, not targets (there are 169 targets under the 17 goals). Therefore, Bangladesh is working to achieve all 17 SDGs.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In 1986, the then foreign minister Humayun Rashid Chowdhury presided over the session of which organ of the UN?
explanation: The United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) is the main deliberative body where all member states have equal representation. Each year, the UNGA holds a regular session, and a President is elected to preside over it. The presidency rotates annually among five regional groups. In 1986, Humayun Rashid Chowdhury, who was the Foreign Minister of Bangladesh, was elected as the President of the 41st Session of the UN General Assembly. This was a significant honor for Bangladesh, reflecting its growing diplomatic stature. The Security Council, Trusteeship Council, and ECOSOC have different leadership structures and functions. Therefore, the organ he presided over was the General Assembly.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Bangladesh has earned international recognition as — i. developed country ii. peace-loving country iii. role model for establishing peace Which one of the following is the correct?
explanation: Bangladesh is classified as a developing country by the United Nations and World Bank, based on indicators like income per capita, human assets, and economic vulnerability. Therefore, statement i is incorrect. However, Bangladesh has built a strong reputation as a peace-loving nation through its consistent support for UN peacekeeping. Since 1988, Bangladesh has contributed over 200,000 personnel to more than 40 peacekeeping missions in 25 countries. Its soldiers are known for professionalism, impartiality, and community engagement. This has earned Bangladesh recognition as a role model in peacekeeping. Thus, statements ii and iii are accurate, making option c the correct choice.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In which month of the year 1979 was the CEDAW convention approved by the UN General Assembly?
explanation: The Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW) went through a thorough drafting and negotiation process involving UN member states and women's organizations worldwide. After final revisions, the text was presented to the United Nations General Assembly for adoption. On December 18, 1979, the General Assembly, meeting in New York, formally approved CEDAW by resolution 34/180. This date is now commemorated annually as International Women's Human Rights Day. The convention was then opened for signature and ratification. Therefore, the month of approval was December.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: Mentionable branch's work are— i. to solve the problem, if any objection remain against the member state of United Nation ii. to dismiss the case in context of any agreement between the member state of the United Nation iii. Change of socio-economic position among the member countries of United Nation Which one is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The question refers to the International Court of Justice (ICJ), the UN's principal judicial organ. Its main functions include settling legal disputes between states. Statement i describes the ICJ's role in adjudicating cases where one member state has a legal objection against another, such as border disputes or treaty violations. Statement ii refers to the ICJ's authority to interpret and apply international agreements, which can involve dismissing cases if they fall outside its jurisdiction or if parties settle. However, statement iii—changing socio-economic positions—is not a function of the ICJ. Socio-economic development is handled by other UN bodies like the General Assembly, ECOSOC, or UNDP. Therefore, only statements i and ii correctly describe the ICJ's work.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: What is the group enjoying the results of development called?
explanation: In the context of the Sustainable Development Goals and modern development approaches, the terminology has shifted from viewing people as passive "beneficiaries" to recognizing them as active "partners." This partnership model emphasizes that communities should be involved in designing, implementing, and evaluating development projects. When people are treated as partners, they have ownership of the outcomes, which leads to more sustainable and equitable results. While "beneficiary" is still used sometimes, the SDG framework specifically promotes partnership as a core principle (SDG 17). "Helpless people" is disempowering, and "development worker" refers to those implementing projects. Therefore, in the partnership method, those enjoying development results are called partners.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background of Establishing the United Nations
question: When was the League of Nations formed?
explanation: The League of Nations was established after World War I as part of the Treaty of Versailles in 1919. However, it officially came into existence on January 10, 1920, when the Covenant of the League of Nations entered into force. Its headquarters were in Geneva, Switzerland. The League aimed to prevent future wars through collective security, disarmament, and peaceful dispute resolution. Despite some early successes, it ultimately failed to stop aggression by major powers in the 1930s, leading to World War II. After the war, it was replaced by the United Nations in 1945. Therefore, the formation year of the League of Nations is 1920.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: What has been explained in the first phase of articles of the CEDAW convention?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"Types of discriminations against women","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"Social rights of the women","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"Economic rights of the women","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"The way of eliminating gender discrimination","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The CEDAW convention is organized into thematic parts. The first part, comprising Articles 1 to 16, focuses on defining and addressing discrimination against women in various spheres. Article 1 provides the key definition of discrimination. Subsequent articles in this section outline specific areas where discrimination must be eliminated, such as political participation, education, employment, healthcare, and family law. This foundational section establishes what constitutes discrimination and sets the scope for state obligations. The later parts cover implementation mechanisms and procedural matters. Therefore, the first phase of articles primarily explains the types of discriminations against women.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In which year the maritime border dispute was resolved between Bangladesh and Myanmar?
explanation: The maritime boundary dispute between Bangladesh and Myanmar in the Bay of Bengal was a complex issue involving territorial waters, exclusive economic zones, and continental shelf rights. After years of unsuccessful bilateral talks, Bangladesh took the case to the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS) in 2009. On March 14, 2012, ITLOS delivered a landmark judgment that delimited the maritime boundary between the two countries. This ruling was widely accepted by both sides and marked a significant victory for Bangladesh, granting it access to substantial maritime resources. Therefore, the dispute was resolved in 2012.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: The main organ of united Nation is —
explanation: The United Nations has six principal organs as per its Charter: the General Assembly, Security Council, Economic and Social Council, Trusteeship Council, International Court of Justice, and Secretariat. Among these, the General Assembly is often considered the main organ because it is the only one where all 193 member states have equal representation and voice. It serves as the primary forum for multilateral discussion on any issue within the UN's scope, sets policies, approves budgets, and elects non-permanent Security Council members. While the Security Council has primary responsibility for peace and security, and the ICJ for legal matters, the General Assembly's universality and broad mandate make it the central deliberative body. UNICEF is a specialized agency, not a principal organ. Therefore, the main organ is the General Assembly.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Achievement of the peace keeping force of Bangladesh is — i. use of Bengali language in Sierra Leone ii. Bangladesh Road has been constructed in Ivory Coast iii. by adopting modern techniques in increasing power Which one is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":false}]
explanation: Bangladeshi peacekeepers have left a positive legacy in mission countries through both security and community engagement. In Sierra Leone, Bangladeshi contingents promoted cultural exchange, and the Bengali language gained some local recognition due to their interactions. In Ivory Coast (Côte d'Ivoire), a road constructed by Bangladeshi engineers was officially named "Bangladesh Road" in honor of their contribution to infrastructure development. These are tangible achievements that built goodwill. However, statement iii about adopting modern techniques for increasing power is vague and not a documented specific achievement of Bangladesh's peacekeeping. Therefore, the verified achievements are i and ii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which organization can ensure the basic right of the children?
explanation: Children's basic rights include survival, development, protection, and participation, as outlined in the Convention on the Rights of the Child. UNICEF (United Nations Children's Fund) is the UN agency specifically mandated to uphold these rights globally. It works to provide immunizations, nutrition, clean water, education, and protection from violence and exploitation. UNDP focuses on broader development, FAO on food and agriculture, and UNESCO on education, science, and culture. While these agencies support children indirectly, UNICEF has the exclusive mandate and expertise to ensure children's basic rights are met. Therefore, UNICEF is the correct answer.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: Which branch of International Organization works for the mutual understanding between 'A' and 'B' state?
explanation: When two states (countries) have a disagreement that they cannot resolve through direct talks, they can seek help from the International Court of Justice (ICJ). The ICJ, based in The Hague, Netherlands, is the UN's principal judicial organ. Its role is to settle legal disputes between states peacefully, based on international law. By providing a neutral forum for adjudication, the ICJ helps build mutual understanding and prevents conflicts from escalating. The General Assembly discusses issues but does not settle specific disputes. The Security Council can intervene in threats to peace but is political, not judicial. The Trusteeship Council is inactive. Therefore, the branch that works for mutual understanding between states through legal resolution is the International Court of Justice.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Bangladesh Peace Keeping troops are contributing to — i. establish a chaos free world ii. give security to some nations iii. develop economy of the world Which one is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":false}]
explanation: Bangladesh sends its brave soldiers and police officers to different countries around the world as part of United Nations Peacekeeping Missions. Let us understand each point clearly: Point i says "establish a chaos free world" - this means Bangladeshi peacekeepers work in war-torn or conflict areas to help stop fighting, reduce violence, and bring stability so that normal life can return. Point ii says "give security to some nations" - this is also true because peacekeepers protect innocent civilians, guard important places, and help local police maintain law and order in countries facing crisis. Point iii says "develop economy of the world" - this is not a direct role of peacekeeping troops. While peace can eventually help economic growth, the main job of peacekeepers is security and stability, not building factories, creating jobs, or managing trade. Therefore, only statements i and ii correctly describe what Bangladesh's peacekeeping forces actually do on the ground.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background of Establishing the United Nations
question: How many powers joined the conference in the year 1943?
explanation: In the year 1943, during the intense period of World War II, four major allied countries came together for an important meeting. These four nations were the United States of America, the United Kingdom, the Soviet Union (now Russia), and China. They are historically known as the "Four Powers". At this conference, these leaders discussed and agreed that after the war ended, the world needed a new international organization where countries could talk to each other peacefully instead of fighting. This important idea eventually led to the creation of the United Nations in 1945. So, the correct number of powers that joined this foundational conference in 1943 was four, making option b the right answer.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Partnership means— i. partners are supposed to do their duties ii. work for self development only iii. work for collective development Which of the following is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":true},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":false}]
explanation: Partnership is a very important concept, especially when working towards big goals like the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Let's break down each statement: Statement i says "partners are supposed to do their duties" - this is absolutely true because in any good partnership, each person or group has responsibilities they must fulfill for the team to succeed. Statement ii says "work for self development only" - this is not correct because partnership is not about thinking only of yourself; if everyone only cared about their own benefit, there would be no real teamwork. Statement iii says "work for collective development" - this is the heart of partnership! It means working together so that everyone benefits, the community grows, and shared goals are achieved. Therefore, partnership combines doing your individual duty (i) while aiming for shared, collective progress (iii), which is why option b is the correct choice.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: When was the charter for abolishing discrimination against women passed?
explanation: The United Nations has worked for many decades to protect women's rights and end unfair treatment based on gender. In the year 1962, the UN General Assembly took a significant step by adopting an important international agreement called the "Convention on Consent to Marriage, Minimum Age for Marriage and Registration of Marriages". This convention was designed to protect women and girls from forced marriages, child marriages, and to ensure that all marriages are properly recorded. While this was not the only document about women's rights (the more comprehensive CEDAW came later in 1979), the 1962 convention was a crucial early charter specifically aimed at abolishing certain forms of discrimination against women in the context of marriage. Therefore, among the given options, 1962 is the correct year.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background of Establishing the United Nations
question: Whom did every country lose due to the 2nd world war?
explanation: World War II, which lasted from 1939 to 1945, was the deadliest conflict in human history. While the war tragically affected people of all ages, one of the most devastating long-term impacts was the massive loss of young adults. These were the men and women in their prime working years - typically between ages 18 and 40 - who were the backbone of their nations' economies, families, and futures. They were the ones who could build homes, grow food, teach children, innovate in science, and lead communities. When millions of these young, capable people died in battle, in bombings, or in concentration camps, countries lost not just lives, but their potential for recovery and growth for many years afterward. While soldiers (option a) were certainly lost, the question asks about what "every country" lost in a broader sense, and the young working generation represents a universal tragedy that affected all nations involved, making option c the most comprehensive and accurate answer.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: The mentionable functions of the organization are... i. providing free books and education materials ii. conducting immunization programme iii. medicare of children Which of the following is correct?
explanation: The organization being described here is UNICEF, which stands for the United Nations Children's Fund. UNICEF has a very broad and important mission to protect and improve the lives of children all over the world. Let's look at each function: Function i - "providing free books and education materials" - Yes, UNICEF works hard to ensure children can go to school by supplying textbooks, notebooks, pencils, and even helping build classrooms in poor or disaster-affected areas. Function ii - "conducting immunization programme" - Absolutely, UNICEF is a global leader in vaccinating children against deadly diseases like polio, measles, and tetanus, often reaching the most remote villages. Function iii - "medicare of children" - This is also correct; UNICEF provides medical care, nutritious food, clean water, and treatment for sick children, especially in emergencies. Since UNICEF actively does all three of these vital activities to help children survive and thrive, the correct answer is d, which includes i, ii, and iii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: Which organ of the United Nations is called the deliberative Assembly?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"The General Assembly","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"The Secretary council","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"The Economic & Social council","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"The Trusteeship council","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The United Nations has several main parts, each with different jobs. The term "deliberative" means a place where representatives come together to discuss, debate, and make decisions on important world issues. The General Assembly is exactly this kind of body. It is like a giant global meeting hall where all 193 member countries of the UN have an equal voice - each country gets one vote. Here, leaders talk about peace, development, human rights, and many other topics. They pass resolutions, set policies, and elect members to other UN bodies. The other options are not primarily deliberative in the same way: there is no "Secretary council" (the Secretariat is the administrative staff), the Economic and Social Council focuses on specific issues like health and education, and the Trusteeship Council (now inactive) handled former colonies. Because the General Assembly is the main forum for worldwide discussion and decision-making, it is rightly called the deliberative assembly of the UN.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: The main objective of SDG is— i. eradication of poverty ii. gender equity iii. economic growth Which of the following is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"i and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":true}]
explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals, or SDGs, are a set of 17 big goals that all United Nations countries agreed to work towards by the year 2030. These goals are designed to make the world a better, fairer, and healthier place for everyone. Let's examine each option: Objective i - "eradication of poverty" - This is Goal Number 1 of the SDGs! Ending poverty in all its forms everywhere is the very first and most fundamental aim. Objective ii - "gender equity" - This is Goal Number 5, which focuses on achieving gender equality and empowering all women and girls, recognizing that half the world's population cannot be left behind. Objective iii - "economic growth" - This is covered under Goal Number 8, which promotes sustained, inclusive, and sustainable economic growth, full and productive employment, and decent work for all. Since all three - ending poverty, ensuring gender equality, and promoting healthy economic growth - are core, official objectives of the SDGs, the correct answer is d, which includes all of them.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background of Establishing the United Nations
question: When did the world see about great devastation and loss of human lives?
explanation: Human history has seen many wars, but one period stands out for its unprecedented scale of destruction and human suffering: World War II. This global conflict officially lasted from the year 1939 to 1945. During these six years, fighting spread across Europe, Asia, Africa, and the oceans. The war involved more countries, used more advanced and deadly weapons, and caused more casualties than any war before it. It is estimated that between 70 to 85 million people lost their lives - including soldiers and millions of innocent civilians who died in bombings, massacres, famines, and the Holocaust. Entire cities were destroyed, economies collapsed, and families were torn apart. The sheer magnitude of this devastation shocked the world and became the primary reason why nations came together after 1945 to create the United Nations - to prevent such a tragedy from ever happening again. Therefore, the period 1939-1945 is the correct answer.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: With the support of UNDP, the sectors where Bangladesh achieved success are — i. maternal health ii. poverty reduction iii. women's empowerment Which one of the following is correct?
explanation: The United Nations Development Programme, known as UNDP, has been a key partner in Bangladesh's development journey for many years. UNDP works with the government and local communities to tackle some of the country's biggest challenges. Let's see how it has helped in each area: Success i - "maternal health" - UNDP has supported projects to train more midwives, improve rural health clinics, and ensure pregnant women get proper care, which has significantly reduced the number of mothers dying during childbirth in Bangladesh. Success ii - "poverty reduction" - Through programs that create jobs, support small farmers, and provide skills training, UNDP has helped millions of Bangladeshis lift themselves out of poverty. Success iii - "women's empowerment" - UNDP actively promotes women's rights by supporting female education, encouraging women to participate in local government, and helping women start their own businesses. Because Bangladesh has made remarkable progress in all three of these areas with UNDP's partnership, the correct answer is d, which includes i, ii, and iii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In which year did the World Health Organisation declare Bangladesh polio free?
explanation: Polio is a very serious and contagious disease that can cause permanent paralysis, especially in young children. For many years, Bangladesh worked very hard, with support from the World Health Organization (WHO) and other partners, to eliminate this disease completely. This involved massive vaccination campaigns where health workers went door-to-door to give polio drops to every child under five years old, even in the most remote villages. After years of sustained effort and careful monitoring, the World Health Organization officially certified Bangladesh as "polio-free" in the month of March in the year 2014. This was a huge achievement for the country's public health system and a testament to the dedication of health workers and communities. Therefore, the correct year is 2014.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: What is the main objective of the SDGs?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"enhancement of local help and co-operation","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"bilateral agreements","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"international co-operation","is_correct":true},{"label":"d","text":"decline of cultural co-operation","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a global blueprint for a better future. While local action is very important, the SDGs recognize that the world's biggest challenges - like climate change, poverty, pandemics, and inequality - cannot be solved by any single country working alone. These problems cross borders and affect everyone. Therefore, the main objective and driving spirit behind the SDGs is to strengthen international cooperation. This means countries sharing knowledge, technology, and resources; working together on research; providing aid to those who need it; and creating fair global rules for trade and finance. Option a (local help) is part of the solution but not the main global objective. Option b (bilateral agreements) is too narrow, as SDGs need multilateral, worldwide effort. Option d (decline of cultural cooperation) is the opposite of what the SDGs want. Thus, international cooperation (option c) is the core objective that makes achieving all 17 goals possible.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: How many secretariats were comprised in the UN?
explanation: The United Nations is a large and complex organization, but it has only one central administrative body called the Secretariat. Think of the Secretariat as the "engine room" or the "office staff" of the UN. It is headed by the Secretary-General and includes thousands of international civil servants who work at UN headquarters in New York and in offices around the world. Their job is to carry out the day-to-day work of the UN: organizing meetings, preparing reports, managing peacekeeping operations, implementing programs, and providing support to all the other UN bodies like the General Assembly and the Security Council. Even though the UN has many different agencies, funds, and programs (like UNICEF, WHO, UNDP), they all operate under the umbrella of the single UN Secretariat for administrative coordination. Therefore, the correct answer is that there is only one Secretariat.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which is the function of FAO?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"To ensure food security","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"To ensure health management","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"To ensure basic rights of the children","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"To foster socio-economic development","is_correct":false}]
explanation: FAO stands for the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations. As its name clearly suggests, this specialized agency has a very specific and vital mission: to defeat hunger and ensure food security for all people worldwide. Food security means that every person, at all times, has physical and economic access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food to meet their dietary needs. The FAO works to achieve this by helping countries improve their farming practices, manage natural resources like water and forests sustainably, develop better fisheries and livestock systems, and respond to food emergencies like famines. While health (option b) is handled by WHO, children's rights (option c) by UNICEF, and broad socio-economic development (option d) by UNDP, the FAO's unique and primary focus is squarely on food and agriculture. Therefore, option a is the correct function of the FAO.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Background and Purpose of the UN
question: The purpose of the agency is — i. to ensure peace ii. to ensure freedom and basic rights of all iii. to build economic, social and cultural cooperation among all nations Which one of the following is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":true}]
explanation: The United Nations was founded in 1945 with a clear and powerful set of purposes written in its founding document, the UN Charter. Let's understand each purpose mentioned: Purpose i - "to ensure peace" - This is the UN's most fundamental goal. The organization was created specifically to prevent another world war by providing a platform for countries to resolve disputes through dialogue and diplomacy instead of violence. Purpose ii - "to ensure freedom and basic rights of all" - The UN is a strong champion of human rights. It adopted the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 1948 and continues to work globally to protect the dignity and freedoms of every individual. Purpose iii - "to build economic, social and cultural cooperation among all nations" - The UN recognizes that lasting peace requires more than just stopping wars; it also needs countries to work together to reduce poverty, improve health and education, and respect different cultures. Since all three purposes - peace, human rights, and international cooperation - are core, officially stated aims of the United Nations, the correct answer is d, which includes all of them.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
board: The Millennium Stars School and College, Rangpur
question: Similar to the group of people, Bangladesh is— i. working to establish a peaceful world ii. defending sovereignty of all countries iii. contributing in international security Which one is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":true},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":false}]
explanation: Bangladesh has earned a wonderful reputation as one of the top contributors of troops and police to United Nations Peacekeeping Missions. Let's analyze each statement carefully: Statement i - "working to establish a peaceful world" - This is absolutely true. Bangladeshi peacekeepers are deployed in conflict zones to help stop fighting, protect civilians, and create conditions for lasting peace. Statement ii - "defending sovereignty of all countries" - This is not the primary role of peacekeepers. The UN operates with the consent of the host country and does not interfere in a nation's internal affairs or defend its sovereignty against external threats; that is the job of a country's own military. Peacekeepers help maintain peace within a country that has invited them. Statement iii - "contributing in international security" - This is correct. By helping to stabilize troubled regions, Bangladeshi peacekeepers contribute to broader international peace and security, which benefits the entire world. Therefore, statements i and iii accurately describe Bangladesh's role in UN peacekeeping, making option b the right answer.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In how many conflict zones are Bangladesh soldiers and police deployed in UN?
explanation: Bangladesh is proudly one of the largest and most respected contributors of personnel to United Nations Peacekeeping Operations. The Bangladeshi military and police forces have served with distinction in many difficult and dangerous locations around the globe. According to official records and reports, Bangladeshi peacekeepers have been deployed to a total of 45 different conflict zones or mission areas. These missions are spread across 11 different countries on multiple continents, including places in Africa, Asia, and the Middle East. Each of these 45 zones represents a specific area where Bangladeshi personnel are actively working to maintain ceasefires, protect civilians, support elections, or help rebuild institutions after conflict. Therefore, the correct number of conflict zones is 45.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which organization is working for the development of the women?
explanation: Several UN agencies work on issues related to women, but each has a slightly different focus. Let's clarify: WHO (World Health Organization) focuses on global public health for everyone. UNICEF (United Nations Children's Fund) works for the rights and well-being of all children, which includes girls but is not exclusively for women. CEDAW is not an organization; it is an important international treaty called the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women. The organization specifically created to work for the development and empowerment of women was UNIFEM, which stands for the United Nations Development Fund for Women. UNIFEM provided financial and technical assistance to innovative programs and strategies that promoted women's human rights, political participation, and economic security. In 2010, UNIFEM was merged with other UN entities to form UN Women, which continues this vital work. Among the given choices, UNIFEM is the correct answer for an organization dedicated to women's development.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: Where is the headquarters of the United Nations situated?
explanation: The United Nations is a global organization, but it has one main central location where its most important meetings take place and where its top leaders, like the Secretary-General, have their offices. This main headquarters is located in the city of New York, in the United States of America. The UN headquarters complex is situated on international territory along the East River in Manhattan. It is here that the General Assembly holds its annual sessions, the Security Council meets to discuss global threats, and many international treaties are signed. While the UN has major offices in other cities like Geneva (Switzerland), Vienna (Austria), and Nairobi (Kenya) for specific agencies and regional work, the official and primary headquarters of the entire United Nations system is in New York City. Therefore, option a is the correct answer.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In Bangladesh, UNHCR has contributed in — i. employment of the refugees ii. bearing the cost of the large number of refugees iii. for providing housing to the Bihari community Which one of the following is correct?
explanation: UNHCR stands for the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees. Its main job is to protect and support people who have been forced to flee their homes due to war, violence, or persecution - these people are called refugees. In Bangladesh, UNHCR has played a significant role in two key areas mentioned: Contribution ii - "bearing the cost of the large number of refugees" - This is a core function of UNHCR. When large numbers of refugees, such as the Rohingya people from Myanmar, arrive in Bangladesh, UNHCR helps provide essential items like food, clean water, shelter, and medical care, and it mobilizes international funding to cover these substantial costs. Contribution iii - "providing housing to the Bihari community" - The Bihari community (also known as stranded Pakistanis) are a group of people who have lived in Bangladesh for decades in difficult conditions. UNHCR has been involved in projects to improve their living conditions, including providing better housing and shelter solutions. Contribution i - "employment of the refugees" - While helping refugees become self-reliant is a long-term goal, providing direct employment is not typically a primary or immediate function of UNHCR; this is more often handled by development agencies like UNDP or the host government. Therefore, the correct contributions are ii and iii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: What are the necessary rights that people need to have for a living called?
explanation: Every person, simply by being human, is entitled to certain fundamental rights that are essential for living with dignity, freedom, and equality. These universal entitlements are called "Human Rights". They are not gifts from any government; they are inherent to all of us, regardless of our nationality, gender, religion, or any other status. Human rights include the right to life and liberty, freedom from slavery and torture, freedom of opinion and expression, the right to work and education, and many more. They are protected by international laws and agreements, such as the Universal Declaration of Human Rights adopted by the United Nations. While terms like "social rights" (a) or "basic rights" (d) might sound similar, "Human Rights" is the specific, universally recognized term used in international law and discourse to describe these essential, inalienable rights that every person needs for a decent life. "Moral rights" (b) is a more philosophical concept and not the standard legal term. Therefore, the precise and correct answer is human rights.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Bangladesh has been successful in resolving the disputes regarding i. the distribution of the water of the Ganges ii. issues of the hill tracts of Chittagong with India iii. 38-year-old maritime dispute with India was resolved Which one of the following is correct?
explanation: Bangladesh has a strong record of using peaceful dialogue and international law to resolve complex disputes with its neighbors. Let's examine each point: Success i - "the distribution of the water of the Ganges" - This is correct. Bangladesh and India signed the historic Ganges Water Sharing Treaty in 1996, which fairly allocates the flow of the Ganges River during the dry season, ensuring water for agriculture and livelihoods in both countries. Success ii - "issues of the hill tracts of Chittagong with India" - This is also correct. The Chittagong Hill Tracts peace accord was signed in 1997 between the Government of Bangladesh and indigenous groups, ending decades of conflict and addressing issues of land rights, autonomy, and development in that region. Point iii - "38-year-old maritime dispute with India was resolved" - While Bangladesh did successfully resolve a long-standing maritime boundary dispute, it is important to note that this dispute involved both India and Myanmar. The boundary with Myanmar was settled in 2012, and the boundary with India was settled in 2014. The statement in the question simplifies this complex history. Given the options and the clear success of the first two points, the most accurate choice based on the provided statements is a, which includes i and ii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: The programmes of this organization are — i. to keep vaccination programme ii. to stop child labour iii. to reduce malnutrition Which one of the following is correct?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"i and ii","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"i and iii","is_correct":true},{"label":"c","text":"ii and iii","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"i, ii and iii","is_correct":false}]
explanation: The organization described here is the World Health Organization, or WHO. WHO is the UN agency responsible for international public health. Let's look at each program: Program i - "to keep vaccination programme" - Yes, this is a major part of WHO's work. WHO leads global efforts to develop, distribute, and administer vaccines to prevent diseases like polio, measles, influenza, and most recently, COVID-19. Program iii - "to reduce malnutrition" - Absolutely, WHO works to combat all forms of malnutrition, including undernutrition in children, vitamin deficiencies, and obesity, by promoting healthy diets and supporting nutrition programs worldwide. Program ii - "to stop child labour" - While ending child labor is a very important goal, it is not primarily WHO's mandate. This issue falls more under the work of the International Labour Organization (ILO) and UNICEF, which focus on labor standards and children's rights respectively. Since WHO's core health programs include vaccination and fighting malnutrition, but not directly stopping child labor, the correct answer is b, which includes i and iii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which World Conference on Women was held in Copenhagen?
explanation: The United Nations has organized a series of major global conferences focused specifically on advancing women's rights and gender equality. These are known as the World Conferences on Women. The very first one was held in Mexico City in 1975. The second conference in this important series took place in the city of Copenhagen, Denmark, in the year 1980. This conference reviewed the progress made since the first one and adopted a Program of Action for the second half of the UN Decade for Women. The third conference was in Nairobi, Kenya (1985), and the fourth and most famous one was in Beijing, China (1995), which produced the landmark Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action. Therefore, the conference held in Copenhagen was the Second World Conference on Women.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: Who is the chief executive of the UN?
explanation: The United Nations is not a world government, so it does not have a "President" in the way a country does. Instead, the top administrative and diplomatic leader of the entire UN system is called the Secretary-General. Think of the Secretary-General as the CEO or chief executive officer of the UN. This person is appointed by the General Assembly upon the recommendation of the Security Council and serves a five-year term. The Secretary-General's responsibilities are huge: they speak for the UN on global issues, propose actions to the Security Council, manage the UN Secretariat staff, and use their "good offices" to mediate international disputes. The current Secretary-General is António Guterres from Portugal. The American President (a) and Japanese Prime Minister (b) are leaders of their own countries, not the UN. Option c, "President", is not a title used for the UN's chief executive. Therefore, the correct title is Secretary-General.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: In African and Asian countries, Bangladesh peacekeepers have received...... from the local people — i. respect ii. love iii. hatred Which one of the following is the correct?
explanation: Bangladeshi peacekeepers are renowned worldwide for their professionalism, discipline, compassion, and effectiveness. When they serve in difficult missions across Africa and Asia, they do much more than just carry out military duties. They build schools and clinics, help with medical camps, teach local children, and interact respectfully with communities. Because of this positive and helpful approach, the local people in the countries where they serve have consistently shown deep appreciation. They express respect (i) for the peacekeepers' skills and integrity, and love (ii) for their kindness and genuine care for the population. The idea of hatred (iii) is completely opposite to the well-documented reality; Bangladeshi contingents are often among the most welcomed and praised by host communities. Numerous reports and testimonials from UN officials and local leaders confirm this positive reception. Therefore, the correct answer is that they have received respect and love, which is option a.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: What type of economy does Bangladesh belong to?
explanation: Countries around the world are often grouped by their economic strength, measured by indicators like Gross National Income (GNI) per person. The World Bank, a major international financial institution, uses these categories: low-income, lower-middle-income, upper-middle-income, and high-income (developed). Bangladesh has made remarkable economic progress in recent decades, with strong growth in industries like garments, agriculture, and remittances from workers abroad. This progress has moved Bangladesh out of the "low-income" category. However, it has not yet reached the level of "upper-middle-income" or "developed" economies. According to the World Bank's current classification, Bangladesh is officially categorized as a "lower-middle-income" economy. This means that while the country is developing rapidly and reducing poverty, there is still significant work to be done to raise the average income and living standards for all its citizens to higher levels. Therefore, option d is the accurate and up-to-date classification.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
board: Shabeed Bh Uttam Li, Anwar Girls' College, Dhaka
question: Which one is the aim to send peace keeping troops to different countries of United Nations? i. To keep human right ii. To form government iii. To keep peace throughout the world Which one is correct?
explanation: United Nations Peacekeeping is a unique and vital tool to help countries torn by conflict move towards lasting peace. Let's understand the aims: Aim i - "To keep human right" - This is a fundamental purpose. Modern UN peacekeeping mandates explicitly include protecting civilians, monitoring human rights situations, and supporting justice and rule of law to ensure that the basic rights of all people are respected during and after conflict. Aim iii - "To keep peace throughout the world" - This is the overarching goal. By deploying neutral troops to monitor ceasefires, separate forces, and build confidence between former enemies, peacekeeping helps prevent local conflicts from escalating and spreading, thereby contributing to global peace and security. Aim ii - "To form government" - This is not a role for UN peacekeepers. The UN respects the sovereignty of nations and does not impose governments. Peacekeepers may help create a secure environment for elections or political processes, but the actual formation of a government is the responsibility of the country's own people. Therefore, the correct aims are i and iii.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: What does UNICEF refer to?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"United Nations Development Programme","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"World Health Organization","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"United Nations International Children's Education Fund","is_correct":true},{"label":"d","text":"United Nations Development Fund for Women","is_correct":false}]
explanation: UNICEF is one of the most well-known and beloved agencies of the United Nations. The name UNICEF originally stood for "United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund" when it was created in 1946 to help children affected by World War II. Although its official name was later shortened to "United Nations Children's Fund" to reflect its broader, ongoing mission, the acronym UNICEF was kept because it was so widely recognized. The option c says "United Nations International Children's Education Fund" - while the word "Education" is not in the official name, UNICEF does place a very strong emphasis on education as a fundamental right for every child. The other options are names of different UN agencies: a) is UNDP, b) is WHO, and d) was UNIFEM (now part of UN Women). Given the choices, option c is the closest and intended reference to UNICEF, highlighting its core focus on children's welfare, which includes education, health, protection, and emergency relief.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: What is the full form of UNIFEM?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"The United Nations Development Fund for Women","is_correct":true},{"label":"b","text":"The United Nations International Children's Education Fund","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"The United Nations Development Programme","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"The World Health Organization","is_correct":false}]
explanation: UNIFEM was a very important specialized agency within the United Nations system that focused exclusively on advancing women's rights and empowerment. The full form of UNIFEM is "The United Nations Development Fund for Women". This name clearly describes its mission: it was a fund that provided financial resources and technical expertise to support innovative programs and strategies that promoted women's economic security, political participation, and freedom from violence. UNIFEM worked in close partnership with governments, civil society organizations, and other UN agencies to turn commitments to gender equality into concrete results on the ground. In 2010, to create a stronger, more unified voice for women within the UN, UNIFEM was merged with three other entities to form a new organization called UN Women, which continues and expands upon UNIFEM's vital work. Therefore, the correct full form is option a.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
lesson: Structure and Organs of the UN
question: The permanent member of the UN Security Council includes— i. France ii. UK iii. Russia Which one of the following is the correct?
explanation: The United Nations Security Council is the most powerful body within the UN, responsible for maintaining international peace and security. It has 15 members in total, but only 5 of them are "permanent members". These five countries were the major allied powers that won World War II and were the principal founders of the UN. They are: China, France, the Russian Federation, the United Kingdom, and the United States of America. Permanent members have a special power called the "veto", which means any one of them can block a substantive Security Council resolution, even if all other members support it. Looking at the options given: i. France - Yes, it is a permanent member. ii. UK (United Kingdom) - Yes, it is a permanent member. iii. Russia - Yes, it is a permanent member, having succeeded the seat originally held by the Soviet Union. Since all three countries listed - France, UK, and Russia - are indeed permanent members of the UN Security Council, the correct answer is d, which includes all of them.

subject: Bangladesh and Global Studies
question: Which of the following is a special side of the SDGs in respect of Bangladesh?
options: [{"label":"a","text":"partnership in a national level","is_correct":false},{"label":"b","text":"international partnership","is_correct":false},{"label":"c","text":"obtaining overall capability","is_correct":false},{"label":"d","text":"participation of the root-level people","is_correct":true}]
explanation: While the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a global framework, each country implements them in ways that fit its own context and priorities. For Bangladesh, one particularly special and important aspect of pursuing the SDGs is the strong emphasis on "participation of the root-level people". This means actively involving ordinary citizens, especially those in villages and poor communities, in the planning, decision-making, and monitoring of development projects. Bangladesh has a rich tradition of grassroots organizations, non-governmental organizations (NGOs), and community-based groups that work directly with people at the local level. This bottom-up approach ensures that development efforts truly address the real needs of the people, are culturally appropriate, and are more sustainable because the community owns the process. While national partnerships (a), international partnerships (b), and building overall capability (c) are also important, the distinctive feature that has contributed significantly to Bangladesh's development success is this deep commitment to including the voices and actions of root-level people in achieving the SDGs.
